items.title: Middle East crisis live: Mojtaba Khamenei chosen as Iran’s new supreme leader; oil prices soar past $100 a barrel
items.standfirst: Iran has named hardliner Mojtaba Khamenei as the country’s new supreme leader; Donald Trump says oil price spike ‘a small price to pay’ as markets tumbleFull report: Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba chosen as Iran’s new supreme leaderTell us: how have you been affected by the latest events in the Middle East?Donald Trump has said a decision on when to end the war with Iran will be a “mutual” one he’ll make together with Benjamin Netanyahu, the Times of Israel has reported.It said Trump also claimed in a brief telephone interview on Sunday that Iran would have destroyed Israel if he and Netanyahu had not been around. The US president said:Iran was going to destroy Israel and everything else around it … We’ve worked together. We’ve destroyed a country that wanted to destroy Israel.I think it’s mutual … a little bit. We’ve been talking. I’ll make a decision at the right time, but everything’s going to be taken into account. Continue reading...
items.content: Iran has named hardliner Mojtaba Khamenei as the country’s new supreme leader; Donald Trump says oil price spike ‘a small price to pay’ as markets tumbleFull report: Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba chosen as Iran’s new supreme leaderTell us: how have you been affected by the latest events in the Middle East?Donald Trump has said a decision on when to end the war with Iran will be a “mutual” one he’ll make together with Benjamin Netanyahu, the Times of Israel has reported.It said Trump also claimed in a brief telephone interview on Sunday that Iran would have destroyed Israel if he and Netanyahu had not been around. The US president said:Iran was going to destroy Israel and everything else around it … We’ve worked together. We’ve destroyed a country that wanted to destroy Israel.I think it’s mutual … a little bit. We’ve been talking. I’ll make a decision at the right time, but everything’s going to be taken into account. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump not ruling out ground troops as Iran war enters second week

items.title: Iran names new supreme leader, 7th U.S. troop dies in war
items.standfirst: Iran has named Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as its new supreme leader. The news come as U.S. Central Command reports a seventh service member has died in the war. Matt Gutman reports.

items.title: Assembly of Experts reaches decision on Khamenei successor
items.standfirst: The body responsible for selecting Iran’s next Supreme Leader said it had reached a decision, though the name has not yet been announced. Meanwhile, Israel warned it would “pursue every successor” to former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israeli airstrikes last weekend. What are the powers and duties of the Iranian Supreme leader? FRANCE24 International affairs editor Shirli Sitbon shares more details on this succession process and on this key figure for Iran.
items.content: The body responsible for selecting Iran’s next Supreme Leader said it had reached a decision, though the name has not yet been announced. Meanwhile, Israel warned it would “pursue every successor” to former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israeli airstrikes last weekend. What are the powers and duties of the Iranian Supreme leader? FRANCE24 International affairs editor Shirli Sitbon shares more details on this succession process and on this key figure for Iran.

items.title: David Petraeus speaks to FRANCE 24: Analysis on US-Israeli strikes on Iran and the regional fallout
items.standfirst: The body responsible for selecting Iran’s next Supreme Leader said it had reached a decision, though the name has not yet been announced. Meanwhile, Israel warned it would “pursue every successor” to former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israeli airstrikes last weekend. Overnight, US and Israeli strikes targeted five oil facilities near Tehran, an Iranian official said. The sites were “damaged,” but the resulting fires were quickly brought under control. Also, Lebanon’s Health Ministry reported that the death toll from Israeli airstrikes over the past week has risen to 394, including many women and children. For a closer look to Iran war, Former US CENTCOM Commander and former CIA Director, General David Petraeus speaks to France 24.
items.content: The body responsible for selecting Iran’s next Supreme Leader said it had reached a decision, though the name has not yet been announced. Meanwhile, Israel warned it would “pursue every successor” to former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in joint US-Israeli airstrikes last weekend. Overnight, US and Israeli strikes targeted five oil facilities near Tehran, an Iranian official said. The sites were “damaged,” but the resulting fires were quickly brought under control. Also, Lebanon’s Health Ministry reported that the death toll from Israeli airstrikes over the past week has risen to 394, including many women and children. For a closer look to Iran war, Former US CENTCOM Commander and former CIA Director, General David Petraeus speaks to France 24.

items.standfirst: Two men are in custody in connection with incident after anti-Islam demonstrators clashed with counterprotestersNew York police have confirmed that an improvised explosive device was thrown outside Zohran Mamdani’s official residence on Saturday when anti-Islam demonstrators, led by rightwing influencer Jake Lang, clashed with counterprotesters.New York police commissioner Jessica Tisch confirmed that a preliminary bomb squad analysis of the device that was ignited and thrown during the protest had “determined that it is not a hoax device or a smoke bomb”. items.title: Iran war drives oil prices above $100 a barrel for first time since 2022
items.standfirst: Donald Trump insists surge in energy prices is ‘very small price to pay’ as Middle East conflict sparks fresh stock market sell-offMiddle East crisis - live updatesOil prices surged past $100 per barrel for the first time since 2022 as fallout from the US-Israel war with Iran continued to rattle global markets and leading economies moved to tackle a worsening energy supply crisis.A weekend of escalating violence in the Middle East intensified concerns around a sustained supply crunch, propelling oil prices to their highest level in four years and triggering a deep stock market sell-off. Continue reading...

items.title: Explosion at US embassy in Oslo may have been terrorism, Norway police say
items.standfirst: Police say they are investigating other motives after the blast caused minor damage to the embassy building.
items.content: Police say they are investigating other motives after the blast caused minor damage to the embassy building.

items.title: Police investigate after explosion outside U.S. Embassy in Norway
items.standfirst: Norwegian police reported on Sunday an explosion near the U.S. Embassy in the capital, Oslo, and said there were no casualties.
items.content: Norwegian police reported on Sunday an explosion near the U.S. Embassy in the capital, Oslo, and said there were no casualties.

items.title: Police search for perpetrators after explosion damages entrance of U.S. Embassy in Oslo
items.standfirst: An explosion at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, Norway, on Saturday caused minor damage but no injuries, police in the Scandinavian nation said.
items.content: An explosion at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, Norway, on Saturday caused minor damage but no injuries, police in the Scandinavian nation said.

clusters.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
clusters.neutral_headline: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Sky News World, BBC World

items.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
items.standfirst: Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled. Continue reading...
items.content: Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled. Continue reading...

items.title: Rapper and Gen Z protest leader on track to be Nepal's PM
items.standfirst: A former rapper is on track to win Nepal’s first election since youth-driven protests toppled the incumbent political leadership last year.
items.content: A former rapper is on track to win Nepal’s first election since youth-driven protests toppled the incumbent political leadership last year.

items.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah unseats Nepal's ex-PM as he heads for victory
items.standfirst: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.
items.content: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.

clusters.title: Why are TSA lines so long? #travel
clusters.neutral_headline: Why are TSA lines so long? #travel
bullet_summary: Reported by Reuters Top News, CBS News

items.title: Why are TSA lines so long? #travel
items.standfirst: The short answer Staffing and operational disruptions tied to an ongoing partial government shutdown have reduced screening capacity at many U.S. airports, producing much longer waits at security checkpoints at major hubs. With fewer screeners on duty, peak‑hour demand outstrips available lanes…
items.content: The short answer Staffing and operational disruptions tied to an ongoing partial government shutdown have reduced screening capacity at many U.S. airports, producing much longer waits at security checkpoints at major hubs. With fewer screeners on duty, peak‑hour demand outstrips available lanes…

items.title: Why are TSA lines hitting three hours? #travel
items.standfirst: What’s driven the surge in airport security waits A partial federal government shutdown has reduced staffing across agencies that support airport operations, creating longer security lines at major U.S. hubs. When positions that process passengers or support checkpoint throughput go unfilled,…
items.content: What’s driven the surge in airport security waits A partial federal government shutdown has reduced staffing across agencies that support airport operations, creating longer security lines at major U.S. hubs. items.title: Long security lines at Houston, New Orleans airports amid lapse in DHS funding
items.standfirst: Wait times to get through security hit two hours in New Orleans and over three hours in Houston as TSA staffing took a hit amid the partial government shutdown.
items.content: Wait times to get through security hit two hours in New Orleans and over three hours in Houston as TSA staffing took a hit amid the partial government shutdown.

clusters.title: 12 years on, renewed hunt for missing Malaysia Airlines flight comes up empty
clusters.neutral_headline: 12 years on, renewed hunt for missing Malaysia Airlines flight comes up empty
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, Sky News World

items.title: 12 years on, renewed hunt for missing Malaysia Airlines flight comes up empty
items.standfirst: Twelve years after Malaysia Airlines Flight MH370 vanished with 239 people aboard, a deep-sea search has so far failed to locate the missing aircraft, as families pressed for the effort to continue.
items.content: Twelve years after Malaysia Airlines Flight MH370 vanished with 239 people aboard, a deep-sea search has so far failed to locate the missing aircraft, as families pressed for the effort to continue.(Image credit: Vincent Thian)

items.title: New update on search for Malaysia Airlines flight MH370
items.standfirst: A renewed deep-sea search for missing Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 has been unsuccessful.
items.content: A renewed deep-sea search for missing Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 has been unsuccessful.

items.title: Video appears to show U.S. cruise missile striking Iranian school compound
items.standfirst: The seven-second video was released by Iranian state media and directly contradicts statements made by President Trump, who said Iran was responsible for the strike.
items.content: The seven-second video was released by Iranian state media and directly contradicts statements made by President Trump, who said Iran was responsible for the strike.(Image credit: Screenshots by Geoff Brumfiel for NPR)

clusters.title: Country Joe McDonald, Woodstock star and anti-war singer, dies aged 84
clusters.neutral_headline: Country Joe McDonald, Woodstock star and anti-war singer, dies aged 84
bullet_summary: His death from complications of Parkinson’s disease was reported by Kathy McDonald, his wife of 43 years, in a statement issued by his publicist
Reported by Guardian World, NPR News

items.title: Country Joe McDonald, Woodstock star and anti-war singer, dies aged 84
items.standfirst: Musician behind Vietnam war protest hit I-Feel-Like-I’m-Fixin’-To-Die Rag dead from Parkinson’s complications, his wife confirms“Country Joe” McDonald, a hippy rock star of the 1960s whose protest track I-Feel-Like-I’m-Fixin’-To-Die Rag rebuked the Vietnam war and became a highlight of the Woodstock music festival, died on Sunday. He was 84.McDonald died in Berkeley, California. His death from complications of Parkinson’s disease was reported by Kathy McDonald, his wife of 43 years, in a statement issued by his publicist. Continue reading...
items.content: Musician behind Vietnam war protest hit I-Feel-Like-I’m-Fixin’-To-Die Rag dead from Parkinson’s complications, his wife confirms“Country Joe” McDonald, a hippy rock star of the 1960s whose protest track I-Feel-Like-I’m-Fixin’-To-Die Rag rebuked the Vietnam war and became a highlight of the Woodstock music festival, died on Sunday. He was 84.McDonald died in Berkeley, California. His death from complications of Parkinson’s disease was reported by Kathy McDonald, his wife of 43 years, in a statement issued by his publicist. Continue reading...

items.title: Country Joe McDonald, anti-war singer who electrified Woodstock, dies at 84
items.standfirst: Country Joe and the Fish's best-known song, "I-Feel-Like-I'm-Fixin'-to-Die Rag," captured the growing anti-war sentiment of the Vietnam era.
items.content: Country Joe and the Fish's best-known song, "I-Feel-Like-I'm-Fixin'-to-Die Rag," captured the growing anti-war sentiment of the Vietnam era.(Image credit: Mario Tama)
